exemplar.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Clear and sustained focus on the prescribed title; purposeful integration of natural sciences and mathematics with relevant, concrete examples.
Coherent, well-structured arguments that engage with alternative perspectives and probe conditions under which tools may both enhance and undermine knowledge.
Effective real-life examples (DNA profiling, base-10 system, graphic calculators) clearly linked to knowledge questions, demonstrating strong application of theory to practice.
Generally well-organized and cohesive essay with a logical progression of ideas and clear signposting between sections.
weaknesses: Evaluation of counterclaims is sometimes cursory and lacks the depth of contrast needed for full critical analysis.
Minor lapses in TOK terminology usage and occasional descriptive treatment of some examples (e.g., intellectual-property limits, Popperâs falsification) prevent exploration at the highest level.
Abrupt transitions in places and some overly long sentences slightly impede overall flow and clarity.
Conclusion restates points but stops short of a decisive final evaluation or broader implications for further inquiry.

annotations.comment: To deepen evaluation, the student could discuss how base-10 influenced other disciplines, illustrating interdisciplinary implications of tools.
Integrate TOK terminology (e.g., 'scope', 'perspective', 'justification') when discussing tool-knowledge relationships to align with assessment criteria.
The conclusion reiterates factors but lacks explicit final evaluation of 'ever-improving tools' in relation to the prescribed title; consider a more decisive ending.
The student correctly quotes and formats the prescribed title at the start, establishing clear focus from the outset.
The thesis clearly outlines factors (improvement, community, ethics, time) and AOKs; this signposting aids coherence.
The discussion of DNA testing would benefit from explicitly linking back to 'ever-improving tools' and critiquing whether each advancement truly yields better knowledge.
The essay would benefit from distinguishing between tangible and intangible tools more explicitly to refine the analysis.
The introduction defines 'tool' and 'knowledge' broadly but lacks connection to the specific knowledge-question phrasing; consider reframing definitions in TOK terminology to sharpen focus.
Effective use of the DNA analysis example illustrates increased reliability in natural sciences; this concrete case grounds the argument well.
The discussion of calculators would benefit from referencing empirical studies on deskilling effects to balance the evaluation.
Insightful temporal analysis highlighting short-term gains versus long-term dependency; this acknowledges time as a dynamic factor.
The JSTOR Daily source is reliable, but the student should provide author credentials and publication context for TOK citations.
Strong analysis connecting increased reliability to falsification processes and a posteriori knowledge; this deepens the argument.
The student should explicitly reference Popperâs terminology (e.g., falsifiability) with proper citation to strengthen the theoretical underpinning.
The caution regarding DNA profile matches introduces nuance but remains descriptive; further evaluation of statistical fallibility rates would enhance depth.
Transitions to the community of knowers are abrupt; craft clearer topic sentences to guide the reader through shifts in perspective.
Insightful discussion on how intellectual property can impede knowledge distribution; this adds critical evaluation of tools beyond the technical.
Some sentences are overly long; consider dividing complex ideas into shorter sentences to enhance clarity and flow.
The IP example lacks quantitative evidence (e.g., cost thresholds, access statistics) which would substantiate claims and improve credibility.
Excellent historical overview of numeral systems culminating in base-10; this example clearly demonstrates tool evolution improving mathematical knowledge.
The impact of graphical calculators on time efficiency is well presented, showing awareness of diverse tool types.
The shift to the mathematics AOK is well signposted, maintaining coherence and reminding the reader of the essayâs structure.
Consider comparing with nonâbase-10 systems (e.g., binary) to illustrate that tool choice can vary across cultures and contexts.
Final summary succinctly restates the perspective but does not address implications for areas of knowledge or prompt further inquiry; a stronger closing would include this.
Reference formatting is inconsistent with TOK guidelines; include author initials and publication dates uniformly.
Web links are overly long; consider using DOIs or shortened URLs to maintain readability and permanence.
Good inclusion of statistical evidence source but lacks publication date in the in-text citation for transparency.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
